title: NOC 21220 – Cybersecurity Specialists | GTR Immigration
description: Cybersecurity Specialists are among the most in-demand IT professionals across Canada, protecting government systems, banks, healthcare networks, and enterprise infrastructure from cyber-threats. Classified under NOC 21220 (TEER 1), this occupation qualifies for Express-Entry-aligned programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), LMIA-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) pathways.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)

duties: Assess physical and technical risks to systems and data
Document security policies and emergency procedures
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, cybersecurity, information systems, or engineering
College diploma in IT or network administration (in some cases)
Vendor-specific security certifications or training (often preferred)
Hands-on experience with monitoring tools, encryption, and incident response
job_outlook: This indicates a large-scale, immigration-relevant labour market, especially favourable for applicants with:
additional_information: Demand continues to rise rapidly due to:
National digital-security strategiesFinancial-sector compliance rulesCloud-infrastructure growthAI-driven cyber-threatsGovernment modernization programs
IT consulting firmsPublic-sector agenciesFinancial institutionsHealthcare networksEnterprise technology departmentsCloud-service providers
Annual salaries generally range between $48,100–$118,500 CAD, depending on province, experience, and security specialization.
